mnemonics: D - Datagram: Data Goes Alone (Datagram stands for Data),
keyPoints: Introduction to Routing Algorithms: A routing algorithm is a set of rules used to forward packets between networks.
Distance-Vector Routing Protocols: DVR (Distance-Vector Routing Protocol) and LSR (Link-State Routing Protocol) are two types of DVR protocols.

Path-Vector Routing Protocols: PVR (Path-Vector Routing Protocol) is not a standard protocol, instead, Unicast Routing Protocols like RIP (Routing Information Protocol) and OSPF (Open Shortest Path First) are used.
Unicast Routing Protocols: RIP (Routing Information Protocol) is a simple distance-vector algorithm used to find the shortest path.
OSPF (Open Shortest Path First) is a link-state routing protocol that uses Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path.
BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) is a complex distance-vector protocol used to route traffic between autonomous systems.
